name: Metafy
description: Marketplace for gamers to book 1:1 coaching, group events, and paid guides from pro players and creators.

details.business: Marketplace / creator platform (consumer-facing coaching + creator tools)
details.targetMarket: Competitive and casual gamers seeking paid coaching, and creators/coaches who want monetization tools

items: 1:1 live coaching sessions (screen share, live play, VOD review)
Subscription tiers for creators (Core vs Pro models)
Events product to host paid live streams/classes

missingFeatures: No strong, standardized coach verification or credentialing beyond profile copy and ratings — creates buyer risk for high-ticket sessions (opportunity to offer verified credentials/endorsements)
Refunds and buyer-protection paths appear inconsistent in reviews — no clear, proactive guarantees for missed or low-quality sessions
Discovery and comparison are seller-driven (wide price variance and bespoke packages) — lacks side-by-side comparison or filters for standardized offerings and outcomes
Limited structured progress tracking or learning analytics for students (no clear curriculum completion metrics or longitudinal performance dashboards)
No obvious enterprise / team APIs or features for esports orgs, coaching academies, or schools to manage many players at scale
Marketplace quality control for new/low-quality coaches (too hit-or-miss) — opportunity for onboarding, training, and certification flows for creators

pricingPlans.name: Core
pricingPlans.note: Free creator tier that pays an 8% commission on earnings; includes ability to sell sessions, guides, and communities.

pricingPlans.name: Pro
pricingPlans.note: Monthly subscription (discounted if billed annually) that lets creators keep 100% of their session and guide revenue and unlocks Pro tools.

pricingEvidence.label: What is Metafy Pro? (Help Center)
pricingEvidence.claim: Metafy Pro subscription costs $29.99/month (or $19.99/month billed annually) and lets creators keep 100% of earnings.

pricingEvidence.label: Account Types: User vs Core vs Pro (Help Center)
pricingEvidence.claim: Core creators pay no monthly fee but Metafy takes an 8% cut; Pro creators pay a monthly fee and keep 100% of earnings.

pricingEvidence.label: Metafy Pro page
pricingEvidence.claim: Marketing page describing Pro benefits, creator tools, and monetization features.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase (company profile)
companyEvidence.claim: Metafy was founded in 2020 and is an online coaching platform for gamers.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase News: Series A coverage
companyEvidence.claim: Metafy closed a $25M Series A (reported); company previously raised additional capital (reported total ~$33M+).

companyEvidence.label: PitchBook / Tracxn summaries
companyEvidence.claim: Third-party business intelligence sites report Metafy headcount in the ~70-100 range and list funding rounds.

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: User reviews report issues such as refund disputes, coach no-shows/reschedules, and mixed support experiences.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit threads (user discussions)
reviewEvidence.claim: Community discussion highlights that quality is hit-or-miss and that buyer due diligence (picking known coaches) matters.

reviewEvidence.label: Sitejabber / community reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Additional consumer reviews pointing to mixed experiences and concerns about coach reliability and refunds.

featureEvidence.label: Our Offerings (Help Center)
featureEvidence.claim: Metafy offers Sessions, Events, Communities, and Guides as core product offerings (1:1 sessions, group events, membership communities, and paid guides).

featureEvidence.label: Esports Insider: product launches
featureEvidence.claim: Metafy launched new products (Groups/Events/Courses) to expand monetization for creators and diversify beyond 1:1 coaching.

featureEvidence.label: Discovery / marketplace (site)
featureEvidence.claim: Discovery pages and coach listings show the marketplace approach (per-game hubs, coach pages, and session listings).

strengths: Comprehensive creator monetization: Sessions, Guides, Communities, and Events let creators diversify revenue without extra platform fees.
Integrated scheduling and payments reduce friction for booking live coaching and group events (one-stop marketplace).
Strong investor backing and recent product launches (Series A coverage) supporting rapid feature expansion and marketing reach.
Clear creator-facing economics (Core 8% vs Pro flat subscription) that accommodate both casual and full-time creators.
weaknesses: Coach quality is inconsistent — platform relies on individual coach quality with limited standardized vetting, producing variable buyer outcomes.
Refunds and customer-support workflows have public complaints, which hurts trust for buyers booking high-priced sessions.
Discovery and pricing are seller-driven with wide variance and few standardized offerings, making comparisons difficult for buyers.
Limited evidence of advanced learning analytics, structured curricula, or longitudinal progress tracking for students.
Few apparent enterprise or API features for organizations wanting to bulk-manage coaching or integrate Metafy into team workflows.
